title: How a ship busted with 5 tonnes of cocaine ended up in Sydney | The World | ABC NEWS
description: A ship that was found to be carrying nearly 5 tonnes of cocaine near French Polynesia earlier this year has been escorted into Sydney Harbour after issuing a distress call. French authorities intercepted the MV Raider in international waters near French Polynesia in January, and allowed the vessel and its 11 crew to continue sailing to Australia after dumping the drugs at sea. Pacific Beat reporter Lucy Cooper has been following the story and spoke with The World's Girish Sawlani about what will happen next to the MV Raider. title: Israel launches ground invasion of southern Lebanon as hundreds of thousands flee | ABC NEWS
description: The Israeli military has launched a major ground offensive against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.  The IDF claims this is only a 'limited' and 'targeted' operation but hundreds of thousands of residents have been displaced following an evacuation order from Israeli authorities.  The move marks a major escalation in the war in the Middle East as Israeli forces continue to trade fire with Hezbollah and Iran. title: Is it time to ration petrol and diesel? | ABC News Daily podcast
description: As the Iran war enters its third week, the ripple effect is growing around the world as the global oil supply through the Strait of Hormuz is disrupted.    In Australia, petrol and diesel prices are soaring and in some regional areas the bowsers have run dry.   Today, David Leaney, an international supply chain expert from the ANU on how panic buying is driving prices higher and why the government should start restricting sales.   Featured:    David Leaney, international supply chain management consultant and Lecturer at Australian National University

There are about 24,000 Australians living in the country and expats say while it is still safe, they are navigating a new normal. title: Federal government releases fuel reserves to counter shortages | ABC NEWS
description: The federal government is releasing 20 per cent of the country’s emergency fuel reserves to ease the pressure from the war in the Middle East on Australia’s oil supply.  Farmers and producers have raised fears petrol shortages could stoke prices of groceries.  Deputy Prime Minister Richard Marles is urging consumers against panic-buying petrol saying the amount of petrol coming into the country remains the same. title: Trump slams European leaders’ reluctance to help open Strait of Hormuz | ABC NEWS
description: US President Donald Trump has hit out at European powers over their reluctance to deploy navies to help unblock the Strait of Hormuz amid the war in the Middle East.  NATO leaders including German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer say their armed forces will only take a defensive stance and will not be ‘dragged’ into the greater conflict with Iran.  Some cargo ships, including an Indian-flagged oil tanker, have passed through the Strait of Hormuz, as Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the crucial shipping passage is only closed to Tehran’s ‘enemies’. title: Criminals using key cloning devices to steal cars in Victoria | ABC NEWS
description: Victoria Police say criminals are using high-tech key cloning devices to steal vehicles as the state grapples with a rise in car thefts. Deputy Commissioner Robert Hill says the devices are legitimately used by mechanics to gain access to locked vehicles. More than 33,000 cars were stolen in the 12 months up to September 2025, with a fifth of those cars never recovered.
